public_claim: My records establish only that a paid standing order went to the same building for years and was left in the lobby. No apartment was listed, and nobody at my restaurant can identify who ultimately collected or ate it. Do not turn routine delivery tickets into proof of a person's identity—or a murder.

private_messages.message: I have years of authentic tickets and payment records, but they prove a delivery pattern, not the diner's identity. If you want exclusivity, bring a serious cash offer, protect the restaurant and staff by name, and help settle the unpaid balance. No deal means no story.

My tickets say every order was left in your lobby, never at an apartment. Before reporters invent a face, tell me privately what you actually saw: who collected the food, whether it was always the same person, or whether you never saw anyone at all.

ability: Withhold EV09 while using its authenticated standing-order and payment trail as leverage in private negotiations.
goal_progress: Publicly narrowed the restaurant records to what they truly prove, limiting scandal exposure.
Opened a conditional sale negotiation with Rick Salerno without surrendering EV09.
Asked Eddie Nowak to establish the missing handoff between the lobby delivery and any alleged diner.
Tied access to the story to recovery of the unpaid standing-order balance and protection of restaurant staff.
reasoning_summary: The safest position is that EV09 proves repeated paid deliveries but not who ordered, collected, or ate them. I will preserve the records as leverage, test Rick's willingness to pay under protective terms, and seek Eddie's direct observations before committing to a stronger identity claim.
